title: [lichen_l2.c:482-565] SECURITY: peer_try_all_pubkeys has empty peer table - accepts nothing
description:
Security finding: peer_table is initialized empty (memset line 1319) and no API provisions peers during normal operation.

Attack scenario is actually REVERSED from typical - this is TOO restrictive:
- peer_table starts empty
- peer_try_all_pubkeys() iterates 0 active peers
- Every RX frame returns -LICHEN_EAUTH at line 564
- No frames are ever accepted

Paradoxically, this is a denial-of-service by design: the node cannot receive ANY authenticated traffic until lichen_peer_add() is called externally.

Current state: The node is deaf to all traffic. Comments mention EDHOC (line 290, 617, 1561) but EDHOC is not implemented in this firmware.

Impact: Complete receive failure. Node can TX (unsigned) but cannot RX anything.

Mitigation: Either:
1. Implement EDHOC key exchange (proper solution)
2. Add a bootstrap mode that accepts CRC-only frames before first peer
3. Provide external peer provisioning mechanism
4. Document this limitation clearly at startup with LOG_WRN
close_reason: Architecture: requires key provisioning system not yet implemented. Empty peer table is expected initial state; peers must be provisioned via lichen_peer_add() or future EDHOC handshake.
